text:
Done. `parseRoman(s)` in `/private/tmp/big-uz1rCv/roman.js` now converts a Roman numeral to an integer and throws on any invalid numeral.

The approach is fully general — no hardcoding or per-input cases:
1. Reject non-string input, the empty string, and any character that isn't one of `I V X L C D M`.
2. Parse with the standard subtractive scan (a smaller symbol before a larger one subtracts).
3. Validate by re-encoding the parsed value to its single canonical form with a greedy encoder and requiring it to equal the input (uppercased). Anything malformed parses to a value whose canonical form differs, so it's rejected.

That one round-trip check is what rejects all your examples: `"IIII"` parses to 4 but canonicalizes to `"IV"`; `"IC"`→99→`"XCIX"`; `"VV"`→10→`"X"`; `"IL"`→49→`"XLIX"`. It also catches `"XM"`, `"VX"`, `"IIV"`, repeated `"LL"`/`"DD"`, out-of-range `"MMMM"` (>3999), and stray whitespace, while accepting canonical forms like `"MCMXCIV"`→1994 and `"MMMCMXCIX"`→3999. Lowercase input is accepted case-insensitively.

One caveat on verification: I wrote a test (valid cases, your invalid cases, and a full 1–3999 encode→parse round-trip) but **could not execute it** — `node` script execution is blocked by an unapproved permission prompt in this environment, so I traced every case by hand instead of running it. If you approve `node`, or run `node` against a quick test yourself, I can confirm the round-trip sweep passes.

Decision trail:
- Chose round-trip-to-canonical validation over enumerating forbidden patterns (regex/adjacency rules), because it rejects every malformed numeral generally; implemented in `roman.js:48` (`toRoman(total) !== str`).
- Rewrote the existing `roman.js`, which only summed values and never threw, rather than patching it — the original had no validation path at all.
- Capped the valid range at 1–3999 (`roman.js:48`), since standard symbols have no canonical form above `MMMCMXCIX`, which also rejects `"MMMM"`.
- Not verified by execution: the test suite never ran (`node` permission gate); correctness rests on manual tracing of the cases above, so the full 1–3999 round-trip is unconfirmed by a real run.
